title: The coffee shop where it all started
snippet: Two lattes at the corner table by the window. Rain outside, warmth inside — a conversation that never really ended.
fullText:
Two lattes at the corner table. Rain tracing lines down the glass, the hiss of the espresso machine, and your laugh cutting through all of it like sunlight.

We talked for three hours — about everything and nothing. You told me about your rock collection and I pretended I knew what schist was. The barista gave us a look at closing time.

I still have the receipt. Crumpled in my wallet like a tiny love letter from the universe that said: pay attention, this one matters.

title: Highway 1, windows down
snippet: No map, no plan — just your playlist, my terrible singing, and 200 miles of coastline ahead of us.
fullText:
Your playlist, my terrible singing, and 200 miles of coastline unspooling ahead. We pulled over six times — once for elephant seals, twice for the light, three times because you screamed "STOP THE CAR" at a rock formation.

Near Bixby Bridge you put your feet on the dashboard and said "I could do this forever." I believed you. I still do.

title: Golden hour at the summit
snippet: The longest day of the year spent chasing the sun. You grabbed my hand at the top so tight I forgot my legs hurt.
fullText:
The longest day of the year, and we spent every hour of sunlight climbing toward it. You carried the snacks. I carried the blanket. We both carried doubts about the last mile.

Then we crested the ridge and the entire Bay Area was laid out below us in gold and shadow, and you grabbed my hand so tight I forgot my legs hurt.

You found a piece of serpentine on the trail and pocketed it with the reverence of someone finding treasure. Because to you, it was.

title: The little Italian place by the water
snippet: Candlelight, homemade pasta, and that look you gave me across the table. The waiter smiled like he knew.
fullText:
A wobbly table, a candle that kept threatening to die, and the best cioppino either of us had ever tasted. The waiter called us "the lovebirds" and brought an extra dessert we didn't order.

You wore that green dress — the one that makes your eyes impossible to look away from. You caught me staring and said "what?" like you didn't already know.

Walking home along the water, you picked up a smooth white pebble and pressed it into my palm. "For the collection," you said.

title: Counting stars from the rooftop
snippet: Blankets, cocoa, and a sky that decided to show off. You found Orion first — then started making up constellations.
fullText:
Blankets piled three deep, thermoses of cocoa that got cold because we forgot to drink them, and a sky that decided to show off just for us.

You found Orion before I could even orient myself. Then Cassiopeia. Then you started making up constellations — "that one's a puppy" — and I loved you so much it physically hurt.

The city went quiet around midnight. We didn't. We whispered about the future like it was a secret only the stars were allowed to overhear.

title: Flour on your nose, love in the air
snippet: The recipe said "simple." The recipe lied. It was the best Valentine's Day either of us ever had.
fullText:
The recipe said "simple." The recipe lied. Within twenty minutes the kitchen looked like a flour bomb went off, the pasta was somehow both undercooked and burnt, and you had sauce in your hair.

It was the best Valentine's Day either of us ever had.

You threw a pinch of flour at me. I threw one back. An hour later we ordered pizza and ate it on the floor surrounded by the beautiful disaster we'd created together.
